headline: EV beats best fixed in 10/10 broker-quality worlds (neutral 0, regression 0).

conditions.name: baseline
conditions.rationale: Canonical Phase 4.1/4.3 world (unposted 0.15, brokers default, reserve center 1.05, win-scale 0.06). The model is trained here; this condition reconciles with the shipped Phase 4.3 EV-vs-fixed ordering.

conditions.name: degraded_corner
conditions.rationale: Everything hostile at once: a no-rate, hotly-contested board served by slow-paying, risky, low-bonded brokers. The decisive test — if the EV policy stays positive here it is not a one-world artifact.

conditions.name: disappearing_loads
conditions.rationale: Loads cover much faster (half-life 18->8h) and contention accelerates coverage harder (factor 4->7). Moves coverage/censoring labels — a calibration stress; the EV oracle (profit-if-won) is coverage-agnostic.

conditions.name: high_contention
conditions.rationale: Contested loads push the broker reserve down much harder (reservation_contention_drop 0.15->0.35): the same ask wins less often on hot loads. Moves the hidden reserve -> a genuine EV-lens stress.

conditions.name: no_rate_heavy
conditions.rationale: Triple the "call for rate" share (unposted 0.15->0.45). Shrinks the priced board and forces the recommender to lean on market-rate anchoring far more often — a direct EV stress on the bid-laddering itself.

conditions.name: risky_brokers
conditions.rationale: A nastier pool: worst-broker default risk doubles (0.16->0.32), bonded share falls (0.45->0.22) and quick-pay thins (0.30->0.12). Shifts the credit/risk features the model scores; realized bid profit is unaffected.

conditions.name: sharp_win_curve
conditions.rationale: The accept/reject threshold around the reserve sharpens (win_logistic_scale_rpm 0.06->0.025): mispricing is punished harder, so bid precision matters more. Stresses whether the EV ladder still lands well.

conditions.name: slow_pay
conditions.rationale: Brokers pay far slower (fast 18->30, slow 55->80 days) and the "late" bar tightens (45->35). Payment timing is orthogonal to realized bid profit, so this mainly stresses model calibration via shifted broker pay features.

conditions.name: tight_brokers
conditions.rationale: Brokers are stingier across the board (reservation_center_mult 1.05->0.92): reserves sit below market, squeezing the profitable win region. The hardest single EV-lens test of margin discipline.

conditions.name: unknown_credit
conditions.rationale: Far more brokers post with paywalled/blank credit (unknown 0.18->0.45). The "unknown" credit bucket the model leans on becomes dominant — a pure covariate-shift stress on calibration, not on EV.
